Analysis Part 1:

  • Show All Code

  • View Source

Descriptive Results of Similarity Ratings

Author

David Izydorczyk

Published

May 23, 2025

Load packages & ggplot theme 
# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(patchwork)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(stringr)
library(tm)
library(ggwordcloud)
library(wordcloud)
library(viridis)



source("../Scripts/plot_settings.R")
source("../Scripts/helper_functions.R")
Load data 
# Load data sets

df_food    <- read_csv2("../Data/3 Food/data_analysis_similarity_food.csv") |>
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=food_stim_1,stim_2=food_stim_2)

df_country <- read_csv2("../Data/1 Countries/data_analysis_similarity_country.csv") |>
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=country_stim_1,stim_2=country_stim_2) 

df_mammal  <- read_csv2("../Data/2 Mammals/data_analysis_similarity_mammals.csv") |> 
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=mammal_stim_1,stim_2=mammal_stim_2) 


# Load stimulus List

stims_food    <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_foods.csv")
stims_country <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_countries.csv") |> rename(category = continent)
stims_mammal  <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_mammals.csv") 


# Add category/continent/family infos to dfs

df_food    <- left_join(df_food,stims_food[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_food[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

df_country <- left_join(df_country, stims_country[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_country[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

df_mammal <- left_join(df_mammal, stims_mammal[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_mammal[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

# Combine into tibble
dfs <- tibble(names   = c("Food", "Countries", "Mammals"),
              dataset = list(df_food,df_country,df_mammal))

Descriptive Statistics

Compute descriptive statistics of similarity ratings 
# Compute average similarities for all pairs
dfs <- dfs |> 
          mutate(sim_mat = purrr::map(dataset, ~ .x |> 
                            group_by(ID_stim_1, ID_stim_2, stim_1, stim_2, category_1, category_2) |> 
                            summarise(norm_sim = mean(norm_sim), .groups = "drop") |> 
                            select(ID_stim_1, ID_stim_2, norm_sim, stim_1, stim_2, category_1, category_2)))



# Compute descriptives per domain
sim_summary <- dfs  |> 
                transmute(names, sim_stats = purrr::map(sim_mat, ~ .x |> pull(norm_sim) |> 
                                                           describe() |> as_tibble()))  |> 
                unnest(sim_stats)


sim_summary |>
  select(names, mean, sd, min, max) |>
  kable(digits = 2)
names mean sd min max
Food 0.17 0.16 0.00 0.99
Countries 0.33 0.15 0.04 0.86
Mammals 0.21 0.19 0.00 0.99

Pairwise Similarity Matrices

Make Figure 3 (Pairwise Similarities) 
# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_food <- dfs$sim_mat[[1]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 0.09444444,
                                     stim_1    = "Traubenzucker",
                                     stim_2    = "Traubenzucker",
                                     category_1 = "Sweets and snacks", 
                                     category_2 = "Sweets and snacks")) |> # To make Traubenzucker appear in the data
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order:

# [1] "Cereal products and potatoes" "Dairy products and eggs"     
# [3] "Fruits"                       "Legumes"                     
# [5] "Meat (substitutes) and fish"  "Nuts"                        
# [7] "Spreads"                      "Sweets and snacks"           
# [9] "Vegetables"

temp_food <- dfs$sim_mat[[1]] |> 
                left_join(order_food, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                left_join(order_food, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_food |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = expression("Stim " * italic(j)),
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Foods") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 5, y = 20, label = "Cereal products \n & potatoes", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 17, y = 30, label = "Dairy products \n & eggs", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 29, y = 35, label = "Fruits", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 37, y = 47, label = "Legumes", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 45, y = 59, label = "Meat (substitutes) \n & fish", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 51, y = 59, label = "Nuts", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 55, y = 64, label = "Spreads", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 61, y = 70, label = "Sweets & \n snacks", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 75, y = 85, label = "Vegetables", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank()) -> sim_mat_plot_food


# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_countries <- dfs$sim_mat[[2]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 99999,
                                     stim_1    = "Vatican City",
                                     stim_2    = "Vatican City",
                                     category_1 = "europe", 
                                     category_2 = "europe")) |> # To make Traubenzucker appear in the data
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order: 
# order_countries$category_1 |> unique()
# [1] "africa"        "asia"          "europe"        "north_america"
# [5] "oceania"       "south_america"

temp_countries <- dfs$sim_mat[[2]] |> 
                     left_join(order_countries, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                     left_join(order_countries, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",
                               suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_countries |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = "",
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Countries") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 7, y = 15, label = "Africa", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 22, y = 30, label = "Asia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 36, y = 45, label = "Europe", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 48, y = 60, label = "North & Central \n America", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 60, y = 70, label = "Oceania", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 74, y = 87, label = "South America", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank())   -> sim_mat_plot_countries



# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_mammals <- dfs$sim_mat[[3]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 99999,
                                     stim_1    = "Asiatischer Elefant",
                                     stim_2    = "Asiatischer Elefant",
                                     category_1 = "Proboscidea", 
                                     category_2 = "Proboscidea")) |>
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1, category_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order: 
# order_mammals$category_1 |> unique()
#  [1] "Artiodactyla"   "Carnivora"      "Cetacea"        "Dasyuromorphia" "Diprotodontia" 
#  [6] "Lagomorpha"     "Mammalia"       "Monotremata"    "Perissodactyla" "Pilosa"        
# [11] "Primates"       "Proboscidea"    "Rodentia"       "Sirenia"     

temp_mammals <- dfs$sim_mat[[3]] |> 
                     left_join(order_mammals, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                     left_join(order_mammals, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",
                               suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_mammals |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = "",
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Mammals") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 7, y = 19, label = "Artiodactyla", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 24, y = 35, label = "Carnivora", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 34.5, y = 43, label = "Cetacea", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 37, y = 52, label = "Dasyuromorphia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 40.5, y = 57, label = "Diprotodontia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 43, y = 60, label = "Lagomorpha", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 45, y = 62, label = "Mammalia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 47, y = 64, label = "Monotremata", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 49.5, y = 68, label = "Perissodactyla", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 52, y = 69, label = "Pilosa", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 61, y = 75, label = "Primates", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 70.5, y = 82, label = "Proboscidea", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 76, y = 85, label = "Rodentia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 79, y = 87, label = "Sirenia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank())  -> sim_mat_plot_mammals



sim_mat_plot_food + sim_mat_plot_countries + sim_mat_plot_mammals +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom',
        legend.key.width=unit(1,"cm")) & 
  scale_fill_continuous(limits = c(0,1),type="viridis")&
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Normalized Similarity    ",
                               label.position = "bottom",
                               title.position = "left", title.vjust = 1,
                               # draw border around the legend
                               frame.colour = "black",
                               barwidth = 15,
                               barheight = 1.5)) 


ggsave("../Figures/pairwise_similarities.pdf", width = 50 , height = 20 , unit = "cm",
       bg="white",dpi=300,device = cairo_pdf)

Table with 5 most similar and dissimilar countries

Table 1 shows the on average 10 most similar and 10 most dissimilar countries.

Make tables 
create_latex_table <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  
  df |> 
    select(stim_1,stim_2,norm_sim) |> 
    arrange(norm_sim) |> 
    mutate(norm_sim = round(norm_sim, 2)) |> 
    slice(c(1:5, (n() - 4):n())) |> 
    mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) %>%  # ensure all are character
    kbl(col.names = c("Stimulus i", "Stimulus j", "M"),align = "c") |> 
    kable_paper(c("hover")) 
  
}



# Apply to all datasets in dfs
dfs <- dfs |> mutate(tbl = map2(sim_mat, names, create_latex_table))

dfs$tbl[[1]] 
dfs$tbl[[2]]
dfs$tbl[[3]]

Descriptive: Features

Figure 2 gives an overview of the features people used to make their similarity judgments. Raw responses where preprocessed (lower case, remove punctuation and white space), lemmatized (i.e., “culture”/“cultural” -> “culture”). Only feature which were reported at least 3 times are plotted.

Plot Wordcloud 
preprocessing <- function(df){
  
  features <- df %>% select(ID, features) %>% distinct()

  # Clean and preprocess the text
  clean_text <- features$features
  
  # Create a text corpus
  corpus <- Corpus(VectorSource(clean_text))
  
  # Convert to lower case
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
  
    # Remove extra whitespace
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)
  
  # Remove punctuation
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
  
  # Remove stopwords (common words that don’t add much meaning)
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("en"))
  
  # Remove other filler words
  words2remove <- c("answer","etc","say","try","two","come","give","similar",
                    "similarity","food","country","animal")
  
  corpus  <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, words2remove)
  

  # Optionally, remove numbers
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
  
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x) textstem::lemmatize_strings(x)))
  
  # Create a term-document matrix
  tdm <- TermDocumentMatrix(corpus)
  
  return(tdm)
  
}

plot_wc <- function(tdm, name){
  
  # Get word frequencies
  word_freqs      <- sort(rowSums(as.matrix(tdm)), decreasing = TRUE)
  word_freq_table <- data.frame(word = names(word_freqs), freq = word_freqs)
  
  # Generate the word cloud again
  ggwordcloud(words    = word_freq_table$word,
            freq     = word_freq_table$freq,
            min.freq = 4, 
            scale    = c(4,0.5), 
            colors   = viridis(8,option = "D"),
            family = "Jost") + 
    labs(title = name) + 
    theme(plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size = 20, face = "bold"),
          panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5, fill = NA))
  
}


dfs %>%
  mutate(tdm     = purrr::map(dataset, preprocessing),
         wc_plot = map2(tdm,names,plot_wc)) |> 
  pull(wc_plot) |> 
  wrap_plots()
  

ggsave("../Figures/feature_wc.pdf", width = 40 , height = 15 , unit = "cm",
       bg="white",dpi=300,device = cairo_pdf)

title: "Analysis Part 1: "
subtitle: "Descriptive Results of Similarity Ratings"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
author: "David Izydorczyk"
format: 
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-copy: true
    code-tools: true
    df-print: kable
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    fig-height: 4
    fig-width:  9
    fig-cap-location: top
    tab-cap-location: top
    embed-resources: true
    grid:
      margin-width: 350px
execute:
  warning: false
  messages: false
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
#| code-summary: "Load packages & ggplot theme"
#| cache: false

# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
library(purrr)
library(patchwork)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(stringr)
library(tm)
library(ggwordcloud)
library(wordcloud)
library(viridis)



source("../Scripts/plot_settings.R")
source("../Scripts/helper_functions.R")
```

```{r data}
#| code-summary: "Load data"

# Load data sets

df_food    <- read_csv2("../Data/3 Food/data_analysis_similarity_food.csv") |>
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=food_stim_1,stim_2=food_stim_2)

df_country <- read_csv2("../Data/1 Countries/data_analysis_similarity_country.csv") |>
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=country_stim_1,stim_2=country_stim_2) 

df_mammal  <- read_csv2("../Data/2 Mammals/data_analysis_similarity_mammals.csv") |> 
                  rename(img_stim_1 = stim_1, img_stim_2 = stim_2,stim_1=mammal_stim_1,stim_2=mammal_stim_2) 


# Load stimulus List

stims_food    <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_foods.csv")
stims_country <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_countries.csv") |> rename(category = continent)
stims_mammal  <- read_csv2("../Materials/stimlist_mammals.csv") 


# Add category/continent/family infos to dfs

df_food    <- left_join(df_food,stims_food[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_food[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

df_country <- left_join(df_country, stims_country[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_country[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

df_mammal <- left_join(df_mammal, stims_mammal[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_1  == ID)) |> 
                left_join(stims_mammal[,c("ID","category")], by =  join_by(ID_stim_2  == ID),suffix = c("_1", "_2"))

# Combine into tibble
dfs <- tibble(names   = c("Food", "Countries", "Mammals"),
              dataset = list(df_food,df_country,df_mammal))

```


# Descriptive Statistics                      

```{r}
#| code-summary: "Compute descriptive statistics of similarity ratings"


# Compute average similarities for all pairs
dfs <- dfs |> 
          mutate(sim_mat = purrr::map(dataset, ~ .x |> 
                            group_by(ID_stim_1, ID_stim_2, stim_1, stim_2, category_1, category_2) |> 
                            summarise(norm_sim = mean(norm_sim), .groups = "drop") |> 
                            select(ID_stim_1, ID_stim_2, norm_sim, stim_1, stim_2, category_1, category_2)))



# Compute descriptives per domain
sim_summary <- dfs  |> 
                transmute(names, sim_stats = purrr::map(sim_mat, ~ .x |> pull(norm_sim) |> 
                                                           describe() |> as_tibble()))  |> 
                unnest(sim_stats)


sim_summary |>
  select(names, mean, sd, min, max) |>
  kable(digits = 2)
```

# Pairwise Similarity Matrices    

```{r}
#| code-summary: "Make Figure 3 (Pairwise Similarities)"
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width:  20
#| fig-align: "center"
#| label: fig-sim
#| fig-cap: "Average Pairwise similarites"
#| column: page

# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_food <- dfs$sim_mat[[1]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 0.09444444,
                                     stim_1    = "Traubenzucker",
                                     stim_2    = "Traubenzucker",
                                     category_1 = "Sweets and snacks", 
                                     category_2 = "Sweets and snacks")) |> # To make Traubenzucker appear in the data
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order:

# [1] "Cereal products and potatoes" "Dairy products and eggs"     
# [3] "Fruits"                       "Legumes"                     
# [5] "Meat (substitutes) and fish"  "Nuts"                        
# [7] "Spreads"                      "Sweets and snacks"           
# [9] "Vegetables"

temp_food <- dfs$sim_mat[[1]] |> 
                left_join(order_food, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                left_join(order_food, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_food |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = expression("Stim " * italic(j)),
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Foods") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 5, y = 20, label = "Cereal products \n & potatoes", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 17, y = 30, label = "Dairy products \n & eggs", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 29, y = 35, label = "Fruits", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 37, y = 47, label = "Legumes", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 45, y = 59, label = "Meat (substitutes) \n & fish", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 51, y = 59, label = "Nuts", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 55, y = 64, label = "Spreads", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 61, y = 70, label = "Sweets & \n snacks", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 75, y = 85, label = "Vegetables", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank()) -> sim_mat_plot_food


# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_countries <- dfs$sim_mat[[2]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 99999,
                                     stim_1    = "Vatican City",
                                     stim_2    = "Vatican City",
                                     category_1 = "europe", 
                                     category_2 = "europe")) |> # To make Traubenzucker appear in the data
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order: 
# order_countries$category_1 |> unique()
# [1] "africa"        "asia"          "europe"        "north_america"
# [5] "oceania"       "south_america"

temp_countries <- dfs$sim_mat[[2]] |> 
                     left_join(order_countries, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                     left_join(order_countries, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",
                               suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_countries |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = "",
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Countries") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 7, y = 15, label = "Africa", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 22, y = 30, label = "Asia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 36, y = 45, label = "Europe", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 48, y = 60, label = "North & Central \n America", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 60, y = 70, label = "Oceania", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 74, y = 87, label = "South America", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5 , angle = 90, lineheight = 0.8) + 
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank())   -> sim_mat_plot_countries



# Reorder stimuli based on categories
order_mammals <- dfs$sim_mat[[3]] |> 
                bind_rows(data.frame(ID_stim_1 = 1, 
                                     ID_stim_2 = 80,
                                     norm_sim  = 99999,
                                     stim_1    = "Asiatischer Elefant",
                                     stim_2    = "Asiatischer Elefant",
                                     category_1 = "Proboscidea", 
                                     category_2 = "Proboscidea")) |>
                arrange(category_1,category_2,stim_1,stim_2) |> 
                transmute(plot_ID   = as.integer(factor(stim_1, levels = unique(stim_1))),
                          plot_stim = stim_1, category_1) |> 
                distinct()

# Category order: 
# order_mammals$category_1 |> unique()
#  [1] "Artiodactyla"   "Carnivora"      "Cetacea"        "Dasyuromorphia" "Diprotodontia" 
#  [6] "Lagomorpha"     "Mammalia"       "Monotremata"    "Perissodactyla" "Pilosa"        
# [11] "Primates"       "Proboscidea"    "Rodentia"       "Sirenia"     

temp_mammals <- dfs$sim_mat[[3]] |> 
                     left_join(order_mammals, by =  join_by(stim_1  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many") |> 
                     left_join(order_mammals, by =  join_by(stim_2  == plot_stim), relationship = "many-to-many",
                               suffix = c("_1", "_2"))
   

temp_mammals |> 
  mutate(plot_ID_min = pmin(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2),
         plot_ID_max = pmax(plot_ID_1, plot_ID_2)) |> 
  arrange(plot_ID_min, plot_ID_max) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = plot_ID_max, y = plot_ID_min, fill = norm_sim)) +
      geom_tile() +
      labs(x = expression("Stim " * italic(i)),
           y = "",
           fill = "Normalized Mean Similarities", title = "Mammals") +
      scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5))) + 
      scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0),breaks = c(1,seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 5)),limits = c(0,100)) + 
      scale_fill_viridis_b() +
      theme_nice() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 7, y = 19, label = "Artiodactyla", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 24, y = 35, label = "Carnivora", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 34.5, y = 43, label = "Cetacea", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) +
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 37, y = 52, label = "Dasyuromorphia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 40.5, y = 57, label = "Diprotodontia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 43, y = 60, label = "Lagomorpha", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 45, y = 62, label = "Mammalia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 47, y = 64, label = "Monotremata", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 49.5, y = 68, label = "Perissodactyla", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 52, y = 69, label = "Pilosa", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 61, y = 75, label = "Primates", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 70.5, y = 82, label = "Proboscidea", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 76, y = 85, label = "Rodentia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 79, y = 87, label = "Sirenia", 
                        size = 5, vjust = 0.5, angle = 90, lineheight = 0.9) + 
      theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
            axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y = element_blank(),
            panel.grid.major = element_blank())  -> sim_mat_plot_mammals



sim_mat_plot_food + sim_mat_plot_countries + sim_mat_plot_mammals +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom',
        legend.key.width=unit(1,"cm")) & 
  scale_fill_continuous(limits = c(0,1),type="viridis")&
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title = "Mean Normalized Similarity    ",
                               label.position = "bottom",
                               title.position = "left", title.vjust = 1,
                               # draw border around the legend
                               frame.colour = "black",
                               barwidth = 15,
                               barheight = 1.5)) 


ggsave("../Figures/pairwise_similarities.pdf", width = 50 , height = 20 , unit = "cm",
       bg="white",dpi=300,device = cairo_pdf)

```

# Table with 5 most similar and dissimilar countries     

@tbl-sim shows the on average 10 most similar and 10 most dissimilar countries.
 
```{r}
#| code-summary: "Make tables"
#| label: tbl-sim
#| tbl-cap: "5 most similar and dissimilar countries (on average)"
#| tbl-subcap: 
#| - "Food"
#| - "Countries"
#| - "Mammals"
#| layout-ncol: 3
#| column: page


create_latex_table <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  
  df |> 
    select(stim_1,stim_2,norm_sim) |> 
    arrange(norm_sim) |> 
    mutate(norm_sim = round(norm_sim, 2)) |> 
    slice(c(1:5, (n() - 4):n())) |> 
    mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) %>%  # ensure all are character
    kbl(col.names = c("Stimulus i", "Stimulus j", "M"),align = "c") |> 
    kable_paper(c("hover")) 
  
}



# Apply to all datasets in dfs
dfs <- dfs |> mutate(tbl = map2(sim_mat, names, create_latex_table))

dfs$tbl[[1]] 
dfs$tbl[[2]]
dfs$tbl[[3]]
```


# Descriptive: Features                          

@fig-wc gives an overview of the features people used to make their similarity judgments. Raw responses where preprocessed (lower case, remove punctuation and white space), lemmatized (i.e., "culture"/"cultural" -> "culture"). Only feature which were reported at least 3 times are plotted.


```{r}
#| code-summary: "Plot Wordcloud"
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width:  14
#| fig-align: "center"
#| label: fig-wc
#| fig-cap: "Wordcloud of reported features"
#| column: page


preprocessing <- function(df){
  
  features <- df %>% select(ID, features) %>% distinct()

  # Clean and preprocess the text
  clean_text <- features$features
  
  # Create a text corpus
  corpus <- Corpus(VectorSource(clean_text))
  
  # Convert to lower case
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
  
    # Remove extra whitespace
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)
  
  # Remove punctuation
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
  
  # Remove stopwords (common words that don’t add much meaning)
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("en"))
  
  # Remove other filler words
  words2remove <- c("answer","etc","say","try","two","come","give","similar",
                    "similarity","food","country","animal")
  
  corpus  <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, words2remove)
  

  # Optionally, remove numbers
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
  
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x) textstem::lemmatize_strings(x)))
  
  # Create a term-document matrix
  tdm <- TermDocumentMatrix(corpus)
  
  return(tdm)
  
}

plot_wc <- function(tdm, name){
  
  # Get word frequencies
  word_freqs      <- sort(rowSums(as.matrix(tdm)), decreasing = TRUE)
  word_freq_table <- data.frame(word = names(word_freqs), freq = word_freqs)
  
  # Generate the word cloud again
  ggwordcloud(words    = word_freq_table$word,
            freq     = word_freq_table$freq,
            min.freq = 4, 
            scale    = c(4,0.5), 
            colors   = viridis(8,option = "D"),
            family = "Jost") + 
    labs(title = name) + 
    theme(plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size = 20, face = "bold"),
          panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5, fill = NA))
  
}


dfs %>%
  mutate(tdm     = purrr::map(dataset, preprocessing),
         wc_plot = map2(tdm,names,plot_wc)) |> 
  pull(wc_plot) |> 
  wrap_plots()
  

ggsave("../Figures/feature_wc.pdf", width = 40 , height = 15 , unit = "cm",
       bg="white",dpi=300,device = cairo_pdf)

```
